question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that generateNextLink() uses last(blocks).index as the next cursor to craft requests that always land on a block boundary, ensuring the next link is always valid and enabling an infinite automated crawl that sustains DB load and degrades >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block number as a hex string (e.g., 0x1) that passes isHexPositiveInt() validation in filterValidityChecks but is routed through getByHashOrNumber() as a number, causing unexpected DB query behavior that when repeated at scale degrades >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block.number=gte:0&order=asc&limit=<maxLimit> from many distributed clients simultaneously, causing the DB to execute many full-forward-scan queries in parallel, saturating DB I/O and degrading >= 30% of mirror node processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: extractOrderByFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with only a block.number filter, causing extractOrderByFromFilters() to select RecordFile.INDEX as the orderBy column, and when combined with a wide block.number range and maxLimit force a full index scan that degrades >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamp=lte:1 (very early timestamp) and order=asc, causing the DB to scan from the earliest block forward looking for blocks with consensus_end <= 1, and when repeated at scale cause unnecessary DB scans that degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a hash value that is a valid 64-char hex string but does not match any stored hash, causing the DB to perform a full LIKE scan that returns no rows, and when repeated at scale with many unique non-matching hashes exhaust DB CPU and degrade >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with block.number=gte:0&timestamp=gte:1&order=asc&limit=<maxLimit> to force the DB to evaluate two range conditions across the full table, and when issued from many clients simultaneously saturate DB CPU and degrade >= 30% of nodes?
